archetype: Real-Time Acoustic-Buoyancy Transient Analyzer for Deep-Water Oil Well Cement Integrity Verification
description: This system ingests high-fidelity acoustic telemetry and pressure data from downhole microphones to detect gas migration and cement bonding failures in real-time during the critical set-time phase of deep-water well completion. It is used by subsea engineering teams to make go/no-go decisions on casing pressure isolation, requiring millisecond-latency processing of non-linear acoustic waveforms to distinguish between fluid movement and valid cement hydration signatures.
rules: MUST implement ISO 13628-5 compliant telemetry handling for real-time multiplexed sensor data to ensure interoperability with existing subsea control modules.
MUST enforce deterministic processing latency under 150ms to trigger automated wellhead valve closures before gas kicks breach the surface.
MUST utilize IEEE 1451 smart sensor self-description protocols to dynamically parse heterogeneous sensor arrays without manual configuration.
MUST integrate with API MPMS Chapter 14.8 standards for acoustic logging interpretation to validate cement bond indices against regulatory thresholds.
MUST maintain an immutable, write-once audit trail of all signal processing states to satisfy DNV-GL certification requirements for forensic well analysis.
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely on batch processing or near-real-time analytics; delayed detection of gas migration in high-pressure, high-temperature (HPHT) environments leads to catastrophic blowouts.

DO NOT use generic cloud-native auto-scaling groups for the inference engine; variable network latency from subsea umbilicals requires edge-computing determinism that public cloud zones cannot guarantee.

DO NOT apply standard signal-to-noise ratio filters designed for consumer audio; subsea acoustic paths require specific attenuation models for seawater density and sediment layers to avoid false negatives.
key_technical_challenges: Solving the inverse problem of acoustic wave propagation through multi-phase fluid-cement-rock interfaces to isolate specific frequency signatures of micro-annuli formation.
Maintaining synchronization between disparate sensor sampling rates (up to 2MHz internal vs. 1kHz telemetry) over low-bandwidth, high-jitter fiber-optic or acoustic modem links.
Implementing fault-tolerant state machine logic that survives total loss of telemetry without data corruption, enabling post-event reconstruction of the cement set curve.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees essential for embedded subsea processors while offering the raw performance needed for real-time DSP.
recommended_stack.framework: Tokio with custom `no_std` HAL adaptations — Enables asynchronous, low-latency I/O handling for sensor streams without the overhead of a full OS runtime.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension) — Optimized for high-write ingestion of time-series sensor data with native support for complex continuous aggregates and retention policies.
recommended_stack.cache: RocksDB embedded cache — Allows for sub-millisecond key-value lookups of sensor calibration coefficients directly on the edge device.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ (ZMQ) — Supports the specific push/pull and pub/sub patterns required for distributing high-frequency data streams to multiple analysis nodes without a central broker bottleneck.
